title: Opentrons COVID-19 testing (Zymo, station  B, 48 samples)
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"1peuo","text":"When placing labware on the OT-2's deck:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"nhbk","text":"Make sure the labware is properly inserted by pressing the corner into the metal springs (pictured below). You should feel a slight click, and the labware should sit completely flat.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7qd2n","text":"Make sure the labware is inserted the right way around, with well A1 at the top left.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":null,"entityRanges":null,"data":{}},{"key":"ahonx","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"4s2sd","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"image","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"6189531F341F11EE9D4302C0B41BC903","height":343,"is_video":false,"legend":"{\"blocks\":[{\"key\":\"rbcq\",\"text\":\"Closeup of gloved hand pressing reagent trough into the labware holding springs\",\"type\":\"unstyled\",\"depth\":0,\"inlineStyleRanges\":[],\"entityRanges\":[],\"data\":[]}],\"entityMap\":{}}","mime":"image/png","nestedSelector":null,"original_name":"Screenshot 2020-03-31 11.00.06.png","placeholder":"https://content.protocols.io/public/5c4b5a51372d7a10e89c75d902fdce443af3e579c86f71aa94f4cc19bcd435f5/b38ce4s6.png","shadow":false,"source":"https://content.protocols.io/public/5c4b5a51372d7a10e89c75d902fdce443af3e579c86f71aa94f4cc19bcd435f5/b38ce4s6.png","width":550}}}}
steps: Clean the OT-2. Cleaning an OT-2 COVID-19 Diagnostic Station by Max Marrone

Place the following labware on the OT-2's deck: Slot 1: An Opentrons Temperature Module with an Opentrons 96 well aluminum block and an empty, sterile NEST 100 µL PCR plate. Slots 3, 6, 7, 9, and 10: A full, sterile rack of Opentrons 200 µL filter tips. Slot 4: An Opentrons Magnetic Module with nothing on it. Slot 11: An empty NEST 1-well reservoir, for organic liquid waste.
Start pre-cooling the Temperature Module to 6 °C .
Wait for the Temperature Module to reach Undetermined .
Place the sample well plate in slot 4 .
Place the reagent reservoir in slot 2.

Double-check all the labware to make sure it looks correct. Check that labware are inserted the right way around (well A1 at the top-left for the plates, well 1 at the left for the troughs). Check that labware are properly clicked into the deck slots. Check the deck layout:
Run the Station B protocol on the OT-2. The protocol file should be StationB-48samples-Zymo.py. Operating an OT-2 for COVID-19 testing by Max Marrone
The output is the NEST 100 µL PCR plate sitting on the aluminum block atop the Temperature Module in slot 1.

Prepare the reagent reservoir. This step is time-sensitive. The MagBinding Beads will slowly settle in the Viral DNA/RNA Buffer. Continue quickly until you start the run on the OT-2. Vortex the Viral DNA/RNA Buffer + MagBinding Beads immediately before adding them to the reservoir. Into a 15 mL NEST 12 well reservoir, pipette or pour the following reagents: Well 1: 14.35 mL, Viral DNA/RNA Buffer + MagBinding Beads Well 2: 14.35 mL, Viral DNA/RNA Buffer + MagBinding Beads Well 3: 14.35 mL, Viral DNA/RNA Buffer + MagBinding Beads Well 4: 13 mL, MagBead DNA/RNA Wash 1 Well 5: 13 mL, MagBead DNA/RNA Wash 1 Well 6: 13 mL, MagBead DNA/RNA Wash 2 Well 7: 13 mL, MagBead DNA/RNA Wash 2 Well 8: 13 mL, 95-100% ethanol Well 9: 13 mL, 95-100% ethanol Well 10: 13 mL, 95-100% ethanol Well 11: 13 mL, 95-100% ethanol Well 12: 5 mL, nuclease-free water A 15 mL NEST 12 well reservoir.
Prepare 3x 14.35 mL, Viral DNA/RNA Buffer + MagBinding Beads ...
In a sterile 15 mL tube, add 14 mL, Viral DNA/RNA Buffer .
Vortex the MagBinding Beads in their container.
Pipe 0.35 mL, vortexed MagBinding Beads to the 15 mL tube.
Repeat for a total of 3x 14.35 mL, Viral DNA/RNA Buffer + MagBinding Beads .
Prepare the sample well plate. The sample well plate is a NEST 96 deep well plate. The 48 samples (#1–#48) should be laid out like this: %s %s For each sample well...
Add 4 µL, Proteinase K .
Add 400 µL, sample .
Mix.
Wait for the run to finish. It should take 2.5–3 hours.
creator: Max Marrone
